children.heading: Executive Director, General Counsel, and other staff
children.label: a Executive Director, General Counsel, and other staff

children.heading: Executive Director
children.label: 1 Executive Director
children.text: The Commission shall have an Executive Director, who shall be paid at a rate not to exceed the rate of basic pay for level V of the Executive Schedule under .section 5316 of title 5

children.heading: Term of service for Executive Director
children.label: 2 Term of service for Executive Director
children.text: The Executive Director shall serve for a term of 4 years. An Executive Director may serve for a longer period only if reappointed for an additional term or terms by a vote of the Commission.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: When a vacancy exists in the position of the Executive Director, the Standards Board and the Board of Advisors shall each appoint a search committee to recommend at least three nominees for the position.

children.heading: Requiring consideration of nominees
children.label: B Requiring consideration of nominees
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C), the Commission shall consider the nominees recommended by the Standards Board and the Board of Advisors in appointing the Executive Director.

children.heading: Interim service of General Counsel
children.label: C Interim service of General Counsel
children.text: If a vacancy exists in the position of the Executive Director, the General Counsel of the Commission shall serve as the acting Executive Director until the Commission appoints a new Executive Director in accordance with this paragraph.

children.heading: Special rules for interim Executive Director
children.label: D Special rules for interim Executive Director

children.heading: Convening of search committees
children.label: i Convening of search committees
children.text: The Standards Board and the Board of Advisors shall each appoint a search committee and recommend nominees for the position of Executive Director in accordance with subparagraph (A) as soon as practicable after the appointment of their members.

children.heading: Interim initial appointment
children.label: ii Interim initial appointment
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (B), the Commission may appoint an individual to serve as an interim Executive Director prior to the recommendation of nominees for the position by the Standards Board or the Board of Advisors, except that such individual’s term of service may not exceed 6 months. Nothing in the previous sentence may be construed to prohibit the individual serving as the interim Executive Director from serving any additional term.

children.heading: General Counsel
children.label: 4 General Counsel
children.text: The Commission shall have a General Counsel, who shall be appointed by the Commission and who shall serve under the Executive Director. The General Counsel shall serve for a term of 4 years, and may serve for a longer period only if reappointed for an additional term or terms by a vote of the Commission.

children.heading: Other staff
children.label: 5 Other staff
children.text: Subject to rules prescribed by the Commission, the Executive Director may appoint and fix the pay of such additional personnel as the Executive Director considers appropriate.

children.heading: Applicability of certain civil service laws
children.label: 6 Applicability of certain civil service laws
children.text: The Executive Director, General Counsel, and staff of the Commission may be appointed without regard to the provisions of title 5 governing appointments in the competitive service, and may be paid without regard to the provisions of chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of that title relating to classification and General Schedule pay rates, except that an individual so appointed may not receive pay in excess of the annual rate of basic pay for level V of the Executive Schedule under section 5316 of that title.

children.heading: Experts and consultants
children.label: b Experts and consultants
children.text: Subject to rules prescribed by the Commission, the Executive Director may procure temporary and intermittent services under by a vote of the Commission.section 3109(b) of title 5

children.heading: Staff of Federal agencies
children.label: c Staff of Federal agencies
children.text: Upon request of the Commission, the head of any Federal department or agency may detail, on a reimbursable basis, any of the personnel of that department or agency to the Commission to assist it in carrying out its duties under this chapter.

children.heading: Arranging for assistance for Board of Advisors and Standards Board
children.label: d Arranging for assistance for Board of Advisors and Standards Board
children.text: At the request of the Board of Advisors or the Standards Board, the Commission may enter into such arrangements as the Commission considers appropriate to make personnel available to assist the Boards with carrying out their duties under this subchapter (including contracts with private individuals for providing temporary personnel services or the temporary detailing of personnel of the Commission).

children.heading: Consultation with Board of Advisors and Standards Board on certain matters
children.label: e Consultation with Board of Advisors and Standards Board on certain matters
children.text: In preparing the program goals, long-term plans, mission statements, and related matters for the Commission, the Executive Director and staff of the Commission shall consult with the Board of Advisors and the Standards Board.
